rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS-JOSEPH MASSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "original" will is a photocopy. If the original will is LOST, the amended petition must be amended again to show that the petitioner seeks to admit a LOST WILL, and the presumption of revocation must be overcome. (2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The amended petition was not e-filed until 7/1/24. (4) The caption of the amended petition is still incomplete if IAEA authority is being requested. (5) Item 3f(2) of the amended petition still has the wrong box checked (no codicil is attached to the amended petition) and appears to be incomplete, as the photocopy of the (lost) will shows that the will was self-proving. (6) Item 3g(2) of the amended petition should not have been checked, as the petitioner seeks appointment as the executor. (7) Item 7 of the amended petition should not have been checked, as Decedent's issue survived. (8) Item 9 of the amended petition is incomplete. (9) The signature of the attorney and signature and verification by the proposed executor are the same as on the original petition; they, therefore, did not sign/verify this amended petition. (10) Not all persons mentioned in the will are listed in item 8 of the petition, and they were not given notice. There are four other grandchildren and two charities (contingent remainder beneficiaries) named in the (lost) will. (11) The proof of service filed on 6/26/24 is also identical to the one filed on 6/4/24 but for the handwritten addition of Saint Anthony Foundation. Notice was clearly not given to Saint Anthony Foundation on 6/3/24. If the original will cannot be found (and filed), a second amended petition for probate of a lost will must be filed, and notice of the second amended petition must be mailed and published AFTER the second amended petition is e-filed. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH CHARLES LAWRENCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural Issues on Wife's Petition: (1) Petition #3.e must be completed as to why the court should waive the bond. No Waivers of Bond on file. Procedural Issue on Objection to Wife' Petition: (1) No proof of service of Objection. Procedural Issues on Daughter's Petition: (1) Proof of publication must be filed. (2) Further allegation re: efforts to locate David, such as a declaration of due diligent search. (3) Petition #8 to list Attorney Mr. William Taggert's information as his name was listed in the will. (4) Need further information regarding the decedent's trust that the will refers to. The trustee may need to be noticed if applicable. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
